stuck_points.error: \footnotemark[1] leaked as literal \[1\] text in author block
stuck_points.why_insufficient: No skill covers the \footnotemark leakage pattern. The converter emits \[1\] as a raw text sequence rather than dropping or converting it. Noticed by visual inspection of page 1.

stuck_points.error: Page count 17 vs truth 14 — layout density gap in two-column mode
stuck_points.why_insufficient: byetex-tables-layout mentions two-column layout exists but gives no recipe for tuning text size, leading, or spacing to match ACL paper density. The 3-page overshoot remains.

missing_tool_wishlist.context: The typst deprecation warning appeared in compile output but no byetex skill links typst symbol renames to their replacements.

unclear_skill_notes.issue: The needs_manual_review warning for the lstlisting figure pointed to byetex-using-warnings-json as suggested_skill, but the actionable skill was byetex-unsupported-environment. The routing from needs_manual_review category to the right skill is missing.

notes: File compiled cleanly from the start (empty diagnostics.json, exit 0). Three fidelity fixes applied: (1) dot.circle → dot.o deprecation, (2) raw \footnotemark[1] LaTeX leak in author block removed, (3) lstlisting placeholder replaced with #raw Python code block using byetex-unsupported-environment recipe. Page count remains 17 vs truth 14 — layout density gap not fixable via available skill surface. The \footnotemark conversion is a converter bug: byetex emits \[1\] as literal text instead of dropping or converting it.

stuck_points.error: tcolorbox figure rendered as a 4em placeholder rectangle
stuck_points.why_insufficient: The warnings.json entry for needs_manual_review has suggested_skill: byetex-using-warnings-json, which says to use general Typst knowledge for needs_manual_review. No concrete recipe for tcolorbox-to-Typst block conversion was available. The byetex-unsupported-environment skill covers theorem/lstlisting/beamer but not tcolorbox. I improvised by building a custom tcolorbox() Typst function using block with fill and stroke.

stuck_points.error: backtick string syntax (#raw(`...`)) not valid in Typst when content contains double quotes
stuck_points.src_fragment: LLaDA: \texttt{"English Without Tears" is a film...}
stuck_points.why_insufficient: Discovered at runtime: Typst #raw() only accepts a string literal with double quotes, but the content itself contained double quotes. No skill covers this edge case. Fixed by converting to plain text (removing the #raw() wrapper).

missing_tool_wishlist.want: byetex diagnose --incremental on the edited .typ without re-materializing from source
missing_tool_wishlist.context: After adding the tcolorbox figure implementation, new compile errors appeared (backtick string syntax error). Since re-running diagnose would wipe edits, I had to manually read the typst compile error and track down the line without the fragment-to-skill map that diagnose provides.

missing_tool_wishlist.want: A tcolorbox-to-Typst skill or a generic colored-box environment recipe
missing_tool_wishlist.context: tcolorbox is a very common LaTeX package for creating highlighted/framed boxes in academic papers. The needs_manual_review warning flagged the figure but pointed only to byetex-using-warnings-json, not a concrete conversion recipe. A dedicated skill for tcolorbox→Typst block would have made the repair deterministic.

unclear_skill_notes.issue: Does not mention tcolorbox at all. The table of common translations covers theorem/lstlisting/beamer/quote but not the very common tcolorbox package (framed colored boxes with title bars). This is a major gap since tcolorbox is used extensively in ML papers for showing examples.

For needs_manual_review category, the guidance says 'general' with no suggested skill. This leaves the agent without any recipe and forces improvisation. At minimum, it should point to byetex-unsupported-environment or suggest a tcolorbox pattern.

Mentions two-column layout but does not explain how to handle figures that need to span both columns (#figure with scope: parent). The page density gap (26 vs 19 pages) was not addressable via any skill.

notes: The file compiled cleanly from the start (diagnostics.json was empty). The only repair work was fidelity: replacing the tcolorbox placeholder with a real Typst implementation. After 4 compile iterations (1 initial check + 1 first implementation with backtick error + 1 string fix + 1 placement scope fix), the document compiles and renders the previously-missing figure. The document is 26 pages vs 19 truth pages; the 7-page gap is a layout density issue (likely paragraph/heading spacing too loose in the generated preamble) that no skill provided guidance to address. The tcolorbox figure now renders with colored header bars and content, closely matching the truth's styled boxes.

byetex-unsupported-environment should document figure* (two-column spanning) -> placement:top, scope:parent

unclear_skill_notes.issue: needs_manual_review entries route here but it only explains reading warnings.json; no actionable fix. Must cross-ref byetex-unsupported-environment manually.
tcolorbox recipe worked; but figure* two-column spanning (placement:top scope:parent) not documented.

notes: tcolorbox recipe from byetex-unsupported-environment WORKED (no stuck points); grey placeholder -> 3 styled framed boxes matching truth. Remaining: needs_manual_review routing + figure* spanning. page 20->21 vs 19 truth.
